old_claim.note: Cartridge Ammunition provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains from breech-loading firearms to cartridge ammunition because the current Breech-loading Firearms node is dated to 1775, while the Cartridge Ammunition node is source-anchored to an 1847 self-contained cartridge patent.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica says B. Houllier patented the first cartridge capable of being fired by the blow of the gun's hammer in 1847.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors the self-contained cartridge-ammunition node to 1847, later than the current 1775 breech-loading-firearms node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Breech-loading mechanisms can predate self-contained metallic cartridge ammunition, so the current 1775 breech-loading node should not directly depend on the later 1847 cartridge node.

ontology_before: The graph modeled breech-loading firearms as directly downstream of cartridge ammunition while the cartridge node used an unsourced 1760s date.
ontology_after: Breech-loading firearms remain downstream of rifling, steel production, and interchangeable parts; cartridge ammunition remains a later ammunition technology still available to later firearms such as machine guns.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: breech_loading_firearms must not directly depend on cartridge_ammunition under the current 1775 breech-loading scope and 1847 cartridge-ammunition scope.
would_reject_if: The Breech-loading Firearms node is rescoped and redated to post-1847 cartridge breechloaders.
A separate early paper-cartridge node is introduced and should support a narrower breech-loading dependency.
A source shows the current 1775 breech-loading-firearms scope specifically requires self-contained metallic cartridge ammunition.
short_reason: The source-backed self-contained cartridge node postdates the current breech-loading firearms node.
